LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MELANIE GRAYSMITH'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT CONVERSE INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL OF LANGUAGE'S ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE DEMURRER SERVED NO USEFUL PURPOSE. ANY FURTHER DETAIL THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS CAN BE OBTAINED THROUGH DISCOVERY. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTION BY THE DEFENDANT IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MELANIE GRAYSMITH'S DEMURRER TO THE ANSWER IS OVERRULED. NO USEFUL PURPOSE IS SERVED BY THE DEMURRER. WHILE THE DEMURRING PARTY MAY BE TECHNICALLY CORRECT AS TO CERTAIN ASPECTS OF THE ANSWER, THE BETTER AND MORE COST-EFFECTIVE APPROACH IS TO SEEK THE INFORMATION THAT IS CLAIMED TO BE LACKING THROUGH DISCOVERY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-13-2012 Vacated

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2012 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2012 Set For Trial On Aug-27-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAMSEY HARAFI OF RUMIN SOMMERS LLP, TEL. NO. 434-4500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ERIN L. MCDERMIT OF THOITS LAW, TEL. NO. (650) 330-4771 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF MELANIE GRAYSMITH'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT CONVERSE INTERNATIONAL SCHOOL OF LANGUAGE'S ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled in its entirety. The demurrer served no useful purpose. Any further detail that plaintiff seeks can be obtained through discovery.
THE REQUEST FOR SANCTION BY THE DEFENDANT IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =302/HK

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2011 Continued To Feb-24-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey A. Snyder, Esq., from Thoits, Love, Hershberger & McLean, 650-327-4200, for Defendant and opposing party

No other appearance. Tentative ruling is adopted.
PLAINTIFF MELANIE GRAYSMITH'S Demurrer to the answer is overruled. No useful purpose is served by the demurrer. While the demurring party may be technically correct as to certain aspects of the answer, the better and more cost-effective approach is to seek the information that is claimed to be lacking through discovery. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
